content.problemStatement:
A set CCC is said to be good if for every 222 distinct elements xxx and yyy belonging to the set, either 2⋅x<y2 \cdot x < y2⋅x<y or 2⋅y<x2 \cdot y < x2⋅y<x is true.

You are given an array AAA of NNN integers where A1≤A2≤…≤ANA_1 \le A_2 \le \ldots \le A_NA1​≤A2​≤…≤AN​. You are also given a parameter SSS, and QQQ queries. In each query:


You are given 222 integers LLL and RRR. Consider the subarray [AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​]. Let us denote this subarray as array BBB.

If S=1S = 1S=1, you need to find the size of the largest good subset of the array BBB.
If S=2S = 2S=2, let KKK denote the size of the largest good subset of the array BBB. You need to find the number of indices iii such that BiB_iBi​ is included in at least one good subset of size KKK.


Note that these good subsets must contain distinct elements only.


Input Format

The first line contains 333 integers - NNN, QQQ and SSS.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next QQQ lines contain 222 integers each - LLL and RRR.


Output Format
For each query, output the required answer.


When S=1S = 1S=1, output the size of the largest good subset of the subarray. When S=2S = 2S=2, output the number of indices which are part of at least one largest good subset.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 1 : The subarray is [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].
Here, [1,3][1, 3][1,3] is a good subset of size 222. This is also the largest since there does not exist a good subset of size 333.
Query 2 : The subarray is [2,3,5,11][2, 3, 5, 11][2,3,5,11].
Here, [2,5,11][2, 5, 11][2,5,11] is a good subset of size 333. There do not exist good subsets of size 444.
Query 3 : The subarray is [1,2,3,5,11,13][1, 2, 3, 5, 11, 13][1,2,3,5,11,13].
Here, [2,5,11][2, 5, 11][2,5,11] is a good subset of size 333. There do not exist good subsets of size 444.

Explanation:
Query 1 : The subarray is [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].
Here, [1,3][1, 3][1,3] is a good subset of size 222. There is no good subset of size 222 using the second element (222), hence the answer is 222.
Query 2 : The subarray is [2,3,5,11][2, 3, 5, 11][2,3,5,11].
Here, [2,5,11][2, 5, 11][2,5,11] is a good subset of size 333. There is no good subset of size 333 using the second element (333), hence the answer is 333.
Query 3 : The subarray is [1,2,3,5,11,13][1, 2, 3, 5, 11, 13][1,2,3,5,11,13].
[1,3,11][1, 3, 11][1,3,11], [1,3,13][1, 3, 13][1,3,13] and [2,5,11][2, 5, 11][2,5,11] are 333 good subsets each of size 333. Combined, they cover all the elements, and hence each element is part of at least one maximal good subset. Thus, the answer is 666.
